The article highlights the inability of these workers to remain indoors due to economic necessity despite the active hostilities and security risks in the region.

content_original: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei said in a late Thursday speech that the Strait of Hormuz, a vital artery for global oil trade, should remain shut.
title_en: Asia-Pacific markets tumble as investors brace for prolonged Middle East war; Iran's Supreme Leader calls for Strait of Hormuz closure
summary_en: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei ordered the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil transit chokepoint, while financial markets in Asia-Pacific declined due to fears of an extended conflict. This directive signals a significant escalation in economic warfare that threatens global energy supplies and heightens the risk of direct military confrontation in the region.

title_original: U.S. Air Force refueler crashes in Iraq while supporting Iran war

title_en: U.S. Air Force refueler crashes in Iraq while supporting Iran war

Iranian regime spreading anti-Israel propaganda across dozens of social media accounts: report
content_original: A new Clemson University report found dozens of social media accounts associated with Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) pushing anti-Israel and anti-U.S. content to sow online discord during the ongoing Iran-U.S. war. According to the report released on Wednesday , at least 62 accounts across X, Bluesky and Instagram were found to have connections to the IRGC despite claiming to be users from the Americas, England, Scotland or Ireland. Though most accounts were less than one year old, some were created as far back as December 2023. "All these accounts systematically amplify politically divisive content and disinformation aligned with IRGC narratives, and they are designed to exploit regional fault lines to advance Iranian regime interests," the report read. BRETT VELICOVICH: IRAN BUILT A DRONE TERROR MACHINE — AMERICA JUST HACKED IT Prior to President Donald Trump's and Israel's military strikes against Iran, most accounts largely focused on divisive domestic positions. H...
title_en: Report: IRGC-linked accounts spread anti-Israel and anti-U.S. propaganda on social media
summary_en: A Clemson University report identified at least 62 social media accounts linked to Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) that are disseminating anti-Israel and anti-U.S. content to sow discord during the ongoing armed conflict. These accounts, posing as users from the Americas and Europe, systematically amplify divisive narratives and disinformation aligned with Iranian military objectives. The activity represents a cyber-information operation by Iran targeting Western and Israeli audiences amidst active hostilities.
